A man sits down before a gun, which is pointed at his head. This is no ordinary gun; i­t's rigged to a machine that measures the spin of a quantum particle. Each time the trigger is pulled, the spin of the quantum particle -- or quark -- is measured. Depending on the measurement, the gun will either fire, or it won't. If the quantum particle is measured as spinning in a clockwise motion, the gun will fire. If the quark is spinning counterclockwise, the gun won't go off. There'll only be a click.

Nervously, the man takes a breath and pulls the trigger. The gun clicks. He pulls the trigger again. Click. And again: click. The man will continue to pull the trigger again and again with the same result: The gun won't fire. text: A big thanks for all the positive comments, and I'm glad a lot of you love this.  But to the naysayers let me make a blanket statement: This is an experiment. I don't believe any other pooper has done this before to themselves (or at least not to this extent).  Yes, I anticipated there would be some negative backlash and unsubs. But I'm having fun.  Those who don't like it don't have to watch.  I could easily go off on an EmperorLemon -style rant, but I don't want the drama.  I have faith that true fans will stay & wait for me to release a YTP of a more preferable source (should be very soon).  I strongly disagree with those who think I'll never again create a classic like Oppa Goddamn Style or IT'S ALL RIGHT HERE AT YOUR FINGERTITS, but everyone is entitled to their own opinion.  In the coming weeks perhaps I can show you I'm still the "funny" cs188 everyone remembers. :)
